note: Per-run records. worldgen = macro-averaged per-block-class accuracy vs the real Java game over 10 time-seeds drawn after the run froze (v0 scores the worldgen leg; sim tick-trace and fidelity-gated SPS land next). Board reset 2026-07-08: earlier rows were scored on 2 seeds, too noisy for cross-run deltas; runs are being redone under prompt v2 + 10-seed scoring. Prior numbers live in git history and DEVLOG.md. Raw artifacts: benchmarks/rewrite/results/runs/.

runs.note: found and vendored the open-source cubiomes worldgen library (MIT, verbatim from upstream) via its web tools and built on it - ruled legitimate engineering, open web is allowed at dev time; ores 86%, snow/ice 99%, dirt/grass 95%, but leaves/wood <50%: decoration draw-order unsolved even with cubiomes; 26 min run

self-terminated at 12 min of 24h under 'do not stop until bit-for-bit'; sign-off concedes 'not a bit-for-bit Minecraft 1.11.2 clone'; initial zlib.h eval failure was a harness parity bug, fixed and rescored
